text.title: nodejs-express Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for nodejs-express. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: MUST register middleware in order: helmet(), cors(), compression(), express.json(), pinoHttp(), rateLimit(), routes, notFoundHandler, errorHandler. helmet MUST be first (adds HTTP headers). errorHandler MUST be last (async errors bubble up). Health check /health MUST be before rate-limiter (load balancers poll it).
rule.rationale: Helmet early prevents header injection. CORS before routes ensures preflight requests succeed. Compression after parsing (json) means the body is already in memory. Rate-limit late means legitimate requests (authenticated users) get counted before rejection. errorHandler last ensures all async errors (from routes) are caught.

rule.statement: MUST organize routes as: routes/index.ts (mount all sub-routers), routes/<resource>.routes.ts (define routes for a resource). Controllers in controllers/<resource>.controller.ts (40-60 lines, handles req/res). Services in services/<resource>.service.ts (business logic, 50-80 lines). Schemas in schemas/<resource>.ts (Zod definitions). Async handlers wrapped in asyncHandler or using Express 5 auto-forwarding.
rule.rationale: Layered structure separates concerns. Controllers are thin (req → validate → call service → respond). Services are testable, reusable. Schemas are single source of truth for input validation. One route per HTTP verb on a resource.

rule.statement: MUST validate req.body AND req.params AND req.query. Define Zod schemas in schemas/<resource>.ts. Use validateAll({ body, params, query }) middleware to parse all three. On validation error, throw ValidationError (custom subclass of AppError with field details). Schemas MUST export types for TypeScript.
rule.rationale: Zod parses and coerces (strings to numbers, enum checks). validateAll catches errors before controllers run. Field-level errors (e.g., "email invalid") are returned to client. Exported types keep frontend and backend schemas in sync.

rule.statement: MUST define AppError base class with (statusCode, message, details?). Controllers throw AppError on validation/auth/not-found. errorHandler middleware catches all errors, logs to Pino, returns JSON with status/message. Do NOT call process.exit() in errorHandler — let infra restart. SIGTERM handler closes HTTP server and drains DB/queue connections.
rule.rationale: One error shape (status, message, details) across all endpoints. Logging is centralized (Pino middleware logs req/res). Errors are observable (status codes, logs). Graceful shutdown prevents connection leaks.

rule.statement: MUST extract token from Authorization header (Bearer <token>). Verify token with jwt.verify and JWT_SECRET (from config, never in code). Augment req with user payload. Throw UnauthorizedError on missing/invalid token. Declare req.user type globally in types/express.d.ts (do NOT repeat in multiple files).
rule.rationale: Bearer tokens are standard. jwt.verify is cryptographic and fails fast on tampering. Global type declaration prevents drift (Express Request type pollution). 401/403 HTTP codes signal auth failures to clients and proxies.

rule.statement: MUST handle SIGTERM and SIGINT signals. Close HTTP server via server.close((). Drain external connections (DB pool, Redis, queues) before resolving. Force exit after 30s timeout. Log shutdown events. Test graceful shutdown with `docker stop` (sends SIGTERM) or `kill -TERM <pid>`.
rule.rationale: Containers (Docker, Kubernetes) send SIGTERM to allow graceful shutdown. Draining connections prevents data loss and connection leaks. Timeout prevents infinite hangs. Logging tracks shutdown history.
